reasoning: The 5-year breakeven inflation rate ended on 2025-11-21 at 2.32%, which is noticeably lower than its recent typical range of 2.36%–2.41% over the past month. A statistical mean-reversion model from FRED suggests a 70% probability of an increase back above 2.32 by late November. However, the last few trading days recorded strong downward momentum (falling from 2.38 to 2.32). Balancing the mean reversion (which pushes the probability above 50%) with the short-term negative momentum, I favor an increase but with caution. Following the instruction that probabilities for distant dates should be closer to 0.5 due to expanding uncertainty, I have staggered the probabilities toward 50% for the longer horizons.
